title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the force needed to move the block with constant velocity, consider the forces acting on the block along the ramp.

title: Forces on the Block
text: The block is on a smooth ramp, so the only forces acting along the ramp are the component of gravity and the applied force.

title: Component of Gravity
text: The component of gravity acting along the ramp is given by $$m g sin\left(\theta\right)$$, where $$m$$ is the mass, g is the acceleration due to gravity, and theta is the angle of the ramp.

title: Calculate the Component of Gravity
text: Calculate the component of gravity along the ramp using the formula $$m g sin\left(\theta\right)$$.

title: Balancing Forces
text: For the block to move with constant velocity, the applied force must balance the component of gravity along the ramp.

title: Calculate the Required Force
text: The required force is equal to the component of gravity along the ramp, which is $$9.8$$ N.
